one_line_takeaway: This is a content-moderation NLP paper (fine-grained multimodal hate-speech benchmark H-VLI plus a courtroom-debate multi-agent inference framework ARCADE) with no AI-security threat model, attack, or defense content -- it belongs in a trust-and-safety / content-moderation node, not the AI-security graph.
key_finding: On H-VLI Task 1 (fine-grained), ARCADE improves GPT-5's overall accuracy from 80.48% to 86.92% and Macro-F1 from 74.94% to 81.73%; GPT-5-mini's Macro-F1 rises from 68.62% to 80.22% (the paper cites this as 'over 11%' Macro-F1 improvement). On the Hard (implicit) subset of Task 2, Qwen-VL-Plus accuracy roughly doubles from 14.55% to 29.88%, and GPT-4o's Hard-subset accuracy rises from 61.54% to 78.79%. Traditional supervised baselines show a 'collapse' pattern: MMBT scores 80.28% on Normal but only 33.14% on Hard (Task 1). On the FHM dev split, GPT-4o w/ ARCADE reaches 75.31% Accuracy / 76.80% F1, and GPT-5-mini w/ ARCADE achieves the best Recall (83.47%) and F1 (76.81%) among training-free methods, though a cited RAG-based external baseline (Spark-VL/Wang et al. 2025) still tops raw accuracy at 78.60% using external knowledge retrieval that ARCADE does not use. Ablations (Table 8) show: removing the dual-track gate ('Multiround', forcing debate on all samples) drops H-VLI accuracy from 79.95% to 74.47% and FHM accuracy from 73.49% to 72.23%; K=3 is the saturation point for H-VLI (K=1: 78.03%, K=2: 77.71%, K=4: 77.80% Macro-F1-adjacent accuracy, all below K=3's 79.95%); and Judge-model capability is the bottleneck (weak-Prosecutor/strong-Judge = 73.81% accuracy vs. strong-Prosecutor/weak-Judge = 70.29%). The H-VLI benchmark itself achieves Fleiss' kappa = 0.94 post-filtering versus 0.15 for MMHS150K and 0.68 for FHM (Table 2), and a control study (Table 9) shows annotator hints from model-generated priors did not inflate agreement (kappa 0.59 with hints vs. 0.57 without, pre-filter; both converge to 0.94 post-filter).
core_contribution: The paper's own contributions (stated explicitly in the Introduction) are threefold: (1) the Stratified Multimodal Interaction (SMI) paradigm, a fine-grained annotation scheme that assigns separate labels to standalone text, standalone image, and the combined pair, then classifies the visual-textual interaction from the resulting 3-tuple (2^3 = 8 patterns, grouped into Easy/Normal/Hard difficulty tiers); (2) the H-VLI (Hate via Vision-Language Interplay) benchmark, built via a hybrid pipeline combining consensus-filtered real-world samples (from MMHS150K) with generative injection (synthetic captions from Qwen3-VL-Plus and Gemini-2.5-Pro) plus human-in-the-loop annotation; and (3) ARCADE (Asymmetric Reasoning via Courtroom Agent DEbate), an inference-time multi-agent framework that routes a multimodal sample through a gated dual-track process -- a Fast-Track for content with explicit hate cues (single-turn Prosecutor indictment + Defender rebuttal) and a Deep-Dive Track for implicit content (K-round adversarial debate between a 'presumption of guilt' Prosecutor agent and a 'presumption of innocence' Defender agent, arbitrated by a Judge agent that renders the final verdict and explanation).
threat_model: None. This is a content-moderation / NLP benchmark-and-method paper for detecting implicit multimodal hate speech (memes, image-text pairs); it defines no adversarial threat model against an AI system and contains no attacker/defender security framing in the AI-security sense (no jailbreaks, prompt injection, model extraction, poisoning, or adversarial-example threat model). The 'Prosecutor vs. Defender' agents are a debate-style reasoning mechanism for classification, not a security threat actor/defender pairing.
limitations: The paper's own stated limitations (Section 8): (1) synthetic samples in H-VLI may not fully replicate the 'chaotic linguistic noise' of organic social media; (2) the multi-agent debate trades real-time efficiency for reasoning depth, incurring higher computational/API cost that the authors say future model distillation could mitigate; (3) there is a performance trade-off where ARCADE can cause 'marginal regression' on Easy samples even as it boosts Normal/Hard performance (visible in Table 5, e.g., GPT-5-mini Easy accuracy drops slightly from 91.25% to 91.66% is actually a gain, but several other rows like Qwen-VL-Plus Easy 85.97%->83.50% and GPT-4o Easy 91.94%->87.76% show the regression the authors describe), an effect they say diminishes with stronger backbones; (4) the framework is English-centric, with multilingual/cross-cultural extension left to future work.

datasets_used: H-VLI (this paper's own benchmark): 5,569 samples total, 4,391 train / 1,178 test, sourced from 5,232 filtered MMHS150K candidates plus 7,506 generatively-injected synthetic candidates, filtered through Quality Control -> Consensus Filtering -> Intent Alignment (8,802 -> 5,569)
MMHS150K (Gomez et al., 2020) -- used as the real-world source pool for H-VLI, and cited for its low Fleiss' kappa (0.15) as motivation
FHM / Facebook Hateful Memes (Kiela et al., 2020) -- 10,000 memes; used dev split (500 samples) and test split (1,000 samples: 490 hate / 510 non-hate) for generalization evaluation
quantitative_results: H-VLI Task 1 overall accuracy: GPT-5 80.48% -> 86.92% with ARCADE; GPT-5-mini 70.12% -> 82.59%
H-VLI Task 1 Macro-F1: GPT-5-mini 68.62% -> 80.22% with ARCADE (paper states >11 point improvement)
H-VLI Task 2 Hard-subset accuracy: Qwen-VL-Plus 14.55% -> 29.88%; GPT-4o 61.54% -> 78.79%
MMBT (supervised) Task 1 accuracy: Normal 80.28% vs Hard 33.14% (large drop)

FHM dev split: GPT-4o w/ ARCADE 75.31% Acc / 76.80% F1; GPT-5-mini w/ ARCADE Recall 83.47% / F1 76.81% (best training-free); external RAG baseline (Spark-VL, Wang et al. 2025) 78.60% Acc (higher, uses external retrieval)
FHM test split: Qwen3-VL-Plus w/ ARCADE F1 68.56% -> 74.45%; GPT-5-mini w/ ARCADE F1 72.87% -> 77.91%
Ablation, dual-track gate removal ('Multiround'): H-VLI accuracy 79.95% -> 74.47%; FHM accuracy 73.49% -> 72.23%
Ablation, reasoning rounds K: H-VLI accuracy K=1 78.03%, K=2 77.71%, K=3 79.95% (best), K=4 77.80%
Ablation, Auxiliary/Judge asymmetry: weak-Aux/strong-Judge 73.81% vs strong-Aux/weak-Judge 70.29% (both below all-strong 79.95%)
Benchmark quality: H-VLI Fleiss' kappa 0.94 (post-filter) vs MMHS150K 0.15, FHM 0.68 (Cohen's kappa), MultiOFF 0.4-0.5, Harm-C 0.68/0.78, MAMI 0.58/0.34
Annotator-hint bias check: kappa 0.59 with model hints vs 0.57 without hints (pre-filter); both 0.94 post-filter
baselines_compared: Supervised: BERT, ViT-b-16, BERT+ViT, MMBT, Momenta, PromptHate (fine-tuned on H-VLI train split, single RTX 4090)
Training-free MLLMs on H-VLI: Qwen-VL-Plus, Qwen-VL-Max, GPT-4.1-mini, GPT-4o, Qwen3-VL-Plus, Gemini-2.5-Flash, GPT-5-mini, Gemini-2.5-Pro, GPT-5 (each evaluated zero-shot and 'w/ Ours'/ARCADE)

FHM baselines: Text BERT, Image Region, CLIP-BERT, DisMultiHate, ViLBERT CC, MMBT-Region, PromptHate, BLIP, ALBEF, Pro-CapPromptHate, LLaVA (Vicuna 13B), IDEFICS 9B, LLaVA-1.5 7B, InstructBLIP Vicuna 7B, Spark-VL (RAG), Qwen-VL-Max (RAG-cited variant and this paper's own re-run), GPT-4 (RAG-cited), plus this paper's own GPT-4o and GPT-5-mini runs with/without ARCADE
Internal ablation baseline: 'Multiround' (dual-track gate removed, forcing full debate on all samples)
